name: West Lake Fish
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon Dry sherry or rice wine
recipeInstructions:
(Sweet-sour Hangchow dish, adapted from Joyce Chen's restaurant)
Other fresh-water fish may be substituted; a small striper may be used in a pinch; look for 15-18 inches (1.5 to 2 lb) fish, preferably one you caught yourself. You could also probably use 2 or 3 bluegills.
Substitute cider vinegar, white wine vinegar, or possibly balsamic
Clean, gut, scale, and rinse the fish. Cut through the skin three diagonal cuts on each side like so ///.
Put the fish, sherry, and ginger slices into a kettle of boiling water. The water should cover the fish. Cover tightly and immediately turn off the heat. Let stand 15 minutes.
Meanwhile, mix all the remaining ingredients and cook over medium-high heat until the sauce thickens. Remove garlic if used. Keep very hot.
Remove the fish carefully from the water. Drain and gently pat dry. Pour the boiling sauce over and serve immediately.
Variation: Cut sugar to 1 tablespoon; cut vinegar to 2 tablespoons. Increase soy sauce to 3 tablespoons; add 2 tablespoons dry sherry to the sauce ingredients; add 2 tablespoons chicken broth to the sauce ingredients. This is no longer West Lake fish but a more standard soy sauce fish.
